title: Compounding Wins

content.statement: Small consistent actions beat rare heroic ones.
content.question: What compounding action are you inconsistent with, and what's the real cost?
sop: Identify one action that compounds over time, content, relationships, savings, skill reps, rather than resetting each time you stop.
Set your Base, Rate, and Time horizon for that action (see calculation, page 5).
Commit to the smallest version of that action you can do daily without fail.
Track your streak visibly, a missed day should be obvious, not easy to ignore.
Review the compounding result quarterly, not daily, daily progress will look small by design.
questions: What compounding action are you inconsistent with, and what's the real cost?
If you'd done your most-skipped habit every single day for the last year, where would you be right now?
Which of your current efforts resets to zero every time you pause it, versus which one keeps its value while you're away?
content.equation_example: Base 100 (for example, email subscribers), Rate 5% growth per month, Time 12 months. Result equals 100 times 1.05 to the 12th power, approximately 180. A higher Rate or longer Time means the compounding effect accelerates dramatically, small consistent rates produce outsized results the longer they run; a broken streak resets the exponent and costs far more than it seems to in the moment.
content.closing_statement: Small and consistent outlasts rare and heroic.

caption:
Principle Nº 107: Compounding Wins

Small consistent actions beat rare heroic ones.

Ask yourself: What compounding action are you inconsistent with, and what's the real cost?
